data.title: Checklists for Grading Object-oriented CS1 Programs: Concepts and Misconceptions
data.abstractNote: In this paper, we begin by considering object-oriented programming concepts and typical novice misconceptions as identified in the literature. We then present the results of a close examination of student programs, in an objects-first CS1 course, in which we find concrete evidence of students learning these concepts while also displaying some of these misconceptions. This leads to the development of two checklists that educators can use when designing or grading student programs.
data.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 12th Annual SIGCSE Conference on Innovation and Technology in Computer Science Education
data.shortTitle: Checklists for Grading Object-oriented CS1 Programs
